name: Adjusted Indirect Scope
description: User-defined indirect spend scope, walking from $388.1M total 2025 indirect to ~$194.8M adjusted via supplier + category exclusions. Rules use the real category names from data.json (some differ in wording from the walk write-up but produce the dollar deltas the user specified). All matchers are case-insensitive on string values. A row is excluded if ANY rule matches (OR semantics).

exclusions.label: Software L4 (non-KPIT)
exclusions.notes: Walk write-up says L4='Software'; the matching real data value is L4='Software/Undefined' which removes exactly $7.86M (matches expected delta to the cent).

exclusions.label: Machinery (Capex) L3
exclusions.notes: Walk write-up says L3='Mechanical Supplies (CAPEX)'; the matching real data value is L3='Machinery (Capex)' which removes exactly $43.55M.

exclusions.label: Supply Chain transportation/logistics
exclusions.notes: Restricted to L2='Supply Chain'; matches the user's name list against EITHER L3 or L4 (data places some of these at L3, others at L4).

exclusions.label: Utilities L4
exclusions.notes: Walk write-up says L4='Utilities'; the matching real data value is L4='Energy And Utilities' which removes exactly $6.20M.

exclusions.label: Engineering Consulting L3
exclusions.notes: Rule removes L3='Engineering Consulting' ($53.62M in isolation). User's expected $32.11M is the walk-incremental impact after the KPIT supplier rule has already fired (KPIT ∩ Eng Consulting overlap = $21.51M).

exclusions.label: Engineering Services subset
exclusions.notes: Includes L3='Machinery (Capex)' which overlaps with the capex_l3 rule. OR-semantics handles this: the row is excluded once. The expected $43.28M is the walk-incremental impact (= non-CAPEX portion of the subset).
